task.title: Reject stored stream version zero explicitly during replay
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/378 by preserving the approved one-based stream-version contract when corrupted or externally modified SQLite rows contain stream_version zero.
acceptance_criteria: Replaying a row whose stream_version is zero returns ReplayError::InvalidStoredVersion(0) with a stable diagnostic.
The invalid stored stream-version error has no underlying source and is returned before typed decoding.
Positive out-of-order stream versions remain VersionGap errors.
The public documentation describes stored versions below one as invalid range failures.
The pinned local and pull-request quality gates pass.

attempts.summary: Added a focused integration test that bypasses SQLite CHECK constraints to persist stream version zero.
attempts.diagnostic: The RED test received stream version gap: expected version 1, found 0, proving replay treated an out-of-domain position as a missing positive position.

attempts.summary: Added an explicit lower-bound guard at the replay conversion boundary.
attempts.patch: After checked i64-to-u64 conversion, replay now maps zero to ReplayError::InvalidStoredVersion before gap detection or event decoding.

attempts.summary: Strengthened the behavior test to prove invalid positions are rejected before decoder dispatch.
attempts.patch: Replayed through a decoder that panics if invoked and asserted the exact error, diagnostic, and absent source.

attempts.summary: Completed independent review and monitored the required pull-request checks.
attempts.diagnostic: Independent pre-commit review found no blocking issues. CodeRabbit passed with one trivial optional consolidation suggestion; the existing checked conversion plus explicit zero guard was retained as the smallest change without introducing an integer cast. Rust quality gate run 29307346539 and CodeRabbit passed; pull request 379 squash-merged as 16c8332.

outcome.summary: Replay now classifies stored stream version zero as an explicit invalid persisted value, preserves positive gap semantics, and rejects the invalid position before typed decoder dispatch.

verification.command: Independent read-only pre-commit review of issue 378 diff # durable evidence: https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/380#issuecomment-4965665213
GitHub Rust quality gate run 29307346539 for pull request 379
CodeRabbit review for pull request 379 # review completed and required status passed
/run/current-system/sw/bin/nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- record validate corpus/development/0014-zero-stored-stream-version.json # result: valid development record
/run/current-system/sw/bin/nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- corpus inspect corpus/development # result: 14 valid, 0 invalid

lessons: Validate persisted values against semantic domain bounds before classifying ordering relationships; representability as u64 alone does not make zero a valid stream position.
Use a panic-on-call decoder in boundary tests to prove replay validation occurs before extensible user decoding code.
Retain positive VersionGap semantics while separating non-positive persisted positions into InvalidStoredVersion.
A direct RED diagnostic makes an error-classification bug observable even when the database schema normally prevents the invalid row.
